senses.definition: plural form of skeletoniser: a person or thing that removes or creates a skeleton structure, often referring to a machine or chemical process that removes flesh from a body or other material to expose a skeleton or framework
examples: The researchers used several skeletonisers in the preparation step.
Skeletonisers in archaeology help reveal the underlying bone structure.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of skeletonise: to remove flesh or outer material to expose a skeleton or to reduce something to its basic framework
examples: The beetle skeletonises the plant tissue.
The process skeletonises the sample for easier study.

senses.definition: present participle of skeletonise: the act of removing flesh to reveal a skeleton or basic structure
examples: The team is skeletonising the remains for analysis.

senses.definition: the process of removing flesh from a body or object to leave a skeleton; also the process of reducing something to its essential structure
examples: Skeletonization of the carcass took several days.
The report discusses the skeletonization of the data for analysis.

senses.definition: to remove flesh from a body or to strip something down to its basic framework
examples: They will skeletonize the specimen before examination.
The software can skeletonize complex shapes for modeling.

senses.definition: having had flesh removed to reveal a skeleton or having been reduced to a minimal or bare structure
examples: The skeletonized remains were carefully studied.
We saw a skeletonized model of the human ribcage.

senses.definition: a device or organism that skeletonizes something; a tool used to remove flesh or to reduce to a skeleton
examples: The skeletonizer removed the soft tissue efficiently.
In art, a skeletonizer helps reveal the underlying structure of the model.

senses.definition: plural of skeletonizer
examples: The factory uses several skeletonizers in the process.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of skeletonize
examples: The program skeletonizes the 3D model for rendering.

senses.definition: present participle of skeletonize
examples: The algorithm is skeletonising complex structures for analysis.

senses.definition: without a skeleton or not having a skeletal structure
examples: The skeletonless ancient creature challenged researchers.

senses.definition: resembling or having the appearance of a skeleton
examples: A skeletonlike frame stood in the corner.

senses.definition: plural of skeleton: the internal framework of bones or a simplified framework of something
examples: The archaeologists found many skeletons.
We built a skeleton of the project to outline its core parts.

senses.definition: a common name for a weed (typically Cirsium arvense or similar) with a dry, thin appearance; often a weed in gardens
examples: We pulled skeletonweed from the flower bed.

senses.definition: having a skeleton-like appearance; thin and bony
examples: A skeletony figure loomed in the doorway.

senses.definition: a Scottish or Northern English dialect word meaning a splinter or small fragment of bone or wood; can also mean a fracture or splinter of bone
examples: A skelf of bone remained embedded.

senses.definition: plural of skelf
examples: The floor was full of skelfs.

senses.definition: a folklore term for a skeletal goose or a bird with a skeletal appearance; very rare/archaic
examples: Legends spoke of a skelgoose in the marshes.

senses.definition: relating to the skeleton; slender or bony
examples: A skelic frame carried the model.

senses.definition: a Scots or Northern English dialect word for a skelf or splinter; sometimes used as a verb meaning to injure with a splinter
examples: A nasty skell of wood scratched his leg.

senses.definition: having a skell-like quality; bony or slender
examples: A skellat shadow crossed the hallway.

senses.definition: a person who skelves (scottish dialect) or a worker who steals bones or wood fragments; dialect usage
examples: The skeller searched the attic for fragments.

senses.definition: a pet name for a person with a lean or skeletal appearance; informal
examples: The skellie stood at the door.

senses.definition: having a skellie-like appearance; pale or thin
examples: A skellied face looked back from the mirror.

senses.definition: a person who is more skellied; comparative form in dialect usage
examples: He looked skellier than before.
